release_notes:
Alpha 4.0.10.42 - 2026-05-13 - Hot-fix: stale masterdata cache
crash on profile load + Slovakia "Rate 0 → E" matcher flexibility.
Pablo (two issues from the same 4.0.10.40/41 session).

Issue 1 — CRASH on profile select
AttributeError: 'Masterdata' object has no attribute
'validation_errors'

Root cause: the SQLite masterdata cache stores pickled
``Masterdata`` instances. We added the ``validation_errors``
field in 4.0.10.38 but didn't bump the cache schema version,
so blobs saved by pre-4.0.10.38 builds came back missing that
field. The GUI tried to read ``md.validation_errors[:10]`` and
crashed during ``_restore_prefs`` → ``_reload_masterdata``.

Three defenses in layers:
1. cache_db.SCHEMA_VERSION 1 → 2. init_db() drops the cache
table on version mismatch (existing comment in cache_db
already wired this behaviour); every user gets a one-time
reparse on first launch with 4.0.10.42.
2. cache_db.get_masterdata() back-fills any missing
dataclass field on unpickled blobs using the live class's
declared defaults / default_factories. Forward-compat:
any future field addition is auto-tolerated without a
schema bump.
3. gui_masterdata_mixin._reload_masterdata reads through
getattr(md, "validation_errors", []) as the last line of
defense.

Issue 2 — Slovakia "Rate 0 → E" matcher too strict
Pablo's SK masterdata uses phrasings the strict three-keyword
matcher rejected: "Reclassify 0 to E", "0 → E", "Replace
zero-rate with E". Since the strict matcher only accepted
text containing "rate" + "0" + "e" simultaneously, none of
these triggered the rule.

Since "Rate 0 → E" is currently the ONLY supported adjustment,
ANY non-empty Adjustments value is now treated as a request to
apply it. The audit side (masterdata_health.py) was loosened
in lockstep — it only warns when neither a zero-concept
("0" / "zero") NOR an 'e' appears anywhere. This catches
accidental notes ("TODO: check with client") without rejecting
every legitimate phrasing.

Forward-compat: when we add more adjustment rules later we'll
introduce keyword routing then.

Tests: +7 in tests/test_410_42_masterdata_cache_and_sk.py
covering the schema bump, the dataclass-field back-fill on a
synthesised stale pickle, the read-side getattr defense, every
SK phrasing Pablo's masterdata uses, empty/whitespace rejection,
audit logic still warning on suspicious notes, and the audit ↔
transformer agreement on legitimate variants. Three obsolete
tests in test_410_41_transformer_truncation_hotfix.py were
rewritten to match the new permissive contract.

Suite: 610 passed, 5 skipped, 0 failures.
